_handle_contract: expected_handles entries are "{external_message_id}#{event_index}". One explicit /note is exactly one EventChunk (I-5 / D-106), so event_index is always 0 — each corpus message resolves to a single "#0" handle whose chunk holds the whole note body. It is NOT a business event id, NOT a Telegram message id, NOT any external domain identifier; it exists only because chunk_id is uuid4 at ingest time and so cannot be pinned in this file directly.
_set_role: OP-5 observability gold set (D-056). Sits beside the frozen D-038 baseline set (eval/retrieval/gold.json + corpus.jsonl); it does not supersede it. Select this set explicitly via the harness --gold/--corpus flags — the default no-flag invocation still loads the D-038 baseline set.
_coverage_axes: OP-5.1 contract — the initial expanded observability set must hold: at least 2 negative queries (empty expected_handles); at least 2 multilingual (Russian) queries tied to Russian corpus lines; at least 3 paraphrase queries whose wording meaningfully diverges from the corpus event text; and a mix of single-hit and multi-hit queries. This is the initial expanded set, not the final coverage target.

queries.query: ходил ли я с котом к ветеринару
did I go skiing this winter
